answers.value:
Mild exercise-induced asthma diagnosed in 2018. I use a blue Ventolin inhaler as needed, mainly before sports or if I start wheezing during more intense physical activity (like running games or basketball). I have never been hospitalised for asthma and have not had a serious attack since primary school, but my parents and I prefer that supervising adults know about it in case I start coughing a lot or seem short of breath.

Known allergy to cashew nuts (not anaphylactic, but I get hives around my mouth and feel nauseous if I accidentally eat them). I carry a non-drowsy antihistamine tablet in my bag when I’m at bigger events or camps, and my parents’ phone numbers should be called if I have any kind of reaction. I always avoid food that clearly contains nuts, but I might need a reminder at shared snack tables.

Otherwise, I am generally healthy, up to date with vaccinations, and do not take any daily medication. I wear contact lenses occasionally for sport and glasses most of the time, so if I seem like I’m squinting at the front, it might just mean I need to switch to my glasses or adjust my contacts.
